assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and an email address they can confirm.
No Paddle sandbox or live account, catalog, client-side token, API key, or notification destination exists yet.
Sandbox testing is acceptable; live website approval is out of scope.

developer_goal: Create a Paddle sandbox account and complete the earliest official first-success path to a working sandbox checkout, webhook flow, or authenticated API result.

first_success.outcome: Unresolved among end-to-end Quickstart checkout-plus-webhooks, five-minute API /event-types success, and SDK or starter-kit first request.
first_success.observable_signal: Get started presents peer Quickstart, SDK, AI-tool, and starter-kit paths; the end-to-end guide ends at Hookdeck webhook events while the API Quickstart ends at a successful /event-types response.

route_selection.surface: Paddle Developer Get started plus sandbox account signup
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete sandbox account-to-first-success route only after the terminal outcome and implementation surface are fixed; do not invent a winner across unequal goals.

candidates.name: End-to-end Quickstart: catalog, CodePen pricing page, checkout, Hookdeck webhooks
candidates.reason: Get started promotes this walkthrough to a pricing page connected to Checkout with webhooks flowing, but it is longer than the API-only first request.

candidates.name: API Quickstart: create API key and GET /event-types
candidates.reason: Official API Quickstart claims first call in about five minutes and returns data with no entities, but it is a different first-success type than a test payment.

candidates.name: Backend SDK language quickstarts, starter kits, or AI-tool routes
candidates.reason: Get started lists Node.js, Python, Go, PHP, starter kits, and AI MCP paths as peer starting points without one corpus-wide default.

required_path.action: Create a Paddle sandbox account from the official sandbox signup entry.

required_fields.label: Personal and business details
required_fields.notes: Sandbox docs require personal and business details; the signup page did not expose a field-level form in the fetch.

required_fields.label: Email confirmation
required_fields.notes: Setup checklist instructs confirming the sandbox email address after signup.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can open the Paddle sandbox dashboard.

required_path.action: Create the credentials required by the selected first-success branch (API key and/or client-side token).

required_fields.label: Credential type for selected branch
required_fields.notes: API Quickstart needs an API key; end-to-end Quickstart needs a client-side token (and later a webhook destination).

required_path.observable_result: Sandbox credentials are available under Developer tools > Authentication.

required_path.action: Complete the selected official first-success branch through its documented terminal.
required_path.observable_result: The selected branch reaches its documented observable terminal.

prerequisites.description: For the end-to-end Quickstart, a browser able to run the getting-started CodePen and an external webhook catcher such as Hookdeck Console.
For the API Quickstart, a client able to call sandbox-api.paddle.com with Bearer authentication.

external_gates.description: Live accounts require approval before real transactions; sandbox is the documented testing path.
End-to-end webhook preview depends on a third-party Hookdeck Console endpoint in the official walkthrough.

unavoidable_waits.description: Sandbox email confirmation is required before the account is fully usable per setup checklist guidance.

platform_outcomes.description: Paddle provisions a sandbox environment separate from live credentials and data.
On the end-to-end branch, overlay checkout accepts published test cards and can emit lifecycle webhooks.
On the API branch, /event-types returns a data array when authentication succeeds.

excluded.item: Live account signup and go-live checklist
excluded.reason: Official guidance recommends sandbox for testing; live requires approval.

excluded.item: Enterprise product tier in the sample catalog
excluded.reason: End-to-end Quickstart creates Starter and Pro only and treats Enterprise as contact-us.

excluded.item: Optional Create an entity customer POST in the API Quickstart
excluded.reason: Docs label creating a customer optional after the /event-types verification request.

sources.title: Get started
evidence_supported: Peer Quickstart, SDK, starter kit, and AI-tool paths

sources.title: Sandbox
evidence_supported: Personal and business details on signup

sources.title: Paddle sandbox signup
evidence_supported: Form fields not enumerable without client-side rendering

uncertainties.question: Which Get started path defines Paddle first success: end-to-end checkout and webhooks, API /event-types, an SDK language quickstart, or a starter kit?
uncertainties.impact: These are peer official starts with different action counts, credentials, and observable terminals.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus policy choice of one bounded developer goal, then verification that its current docs form a single complete sandbox path.

uncertainties.question: What personal and business fields are currently required on sandbox signup?
uncertainties.impact: Required field counts cannot be verified without a complete public form enumeration.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated screenshot or read-only walkthrough of sandbox-login.paddle.com/signup field controls.
